cause_of_action: OTHER CIVIL PETITIONS (OF SHIRLEY BATTS TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY)

docket_entries.description: ASBESTOS DISCOVERY 220, PETITIONER SHIRLEY BATTS' PETITION TO PERPETUATE TESTIMONY OF JIM BATTS, JR. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. C.C.P. 2035.010 ALLOWS A PERSON WHO EXPECTS TO BE A PARTY TO AN ACTION TO OBTAIN DISCOVERY FOR THE PURPOSE OF PERPETUATING THE TESTIMONY OF ANOTHER PERSON. PETITIONER'S REQUEST TO HAVE MR. BATTS' DEPOSITION HELD AT OR NEAR HIS HOME IN RUSSELLVILLE, ARKANSAS IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Shirley Batts To Perpetuate Testimony Of Jim Batts, Jr By Videotaped Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for petitioner / moving party Shirley Batts

Paige A. Kremser Stenrud, Esq., of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for respondent Nissan North America

Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for respondent Dana Corporation

Kurt Putnam, Esq. of HASSARD BONNINGTON (415-288-9800), appearing for respondent John Crane Inc.

Beau Lafayette Epperly, Esq. of POND NORTH (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant Orchard


RULING:

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Petitioner Shirley Batts' petition to perpetuate testimony of Jim Batts, Jr. is granted. C.C.P. 2035.010 allows a person who expects to be a party to an action to obtain discovery for the purpose of perpetuating the testimony of another person. Petitioner's request to have Mr. Batts' deposition held at or near his home in Russellville, Arkansas is granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported. =(220/HEK)
